audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for coalition war narrative, Lot capture/rescue, Abram as Hebrew, Melchizedek priest-king material, El Elyon, blessing/tithe language, and Abram's refusal of Sodom's wealth. Final source-critical review may refine names and geography.

verses.restored: In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim,

familiar.text: And it came to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar...

notes.title: Kings and lands
notes.body: The chapter opens as an international coalition narrative, full of ancient royal names and places.

verses.restored: they made war with Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, that is, Zoar.

familiar.text: That these made war with Bera king of Sodom...

verses.restored: All these joined together in the Valley of Siddim, that is, the Salt Sea.

familiar.text: All these were joined together in the vale of Siddim, which is the salt sea.

verses.restored: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

familiar.text: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

notes.title: Served and rebelled
notes.body: The conflict is framed around vassal service and rebellion against imperial dominance.

verses.restored: In the fourteenth year, Chedorlaomer and the kings with him came and struck the Rephaim in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-Kiriathaim,

familiar.text: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer... and smote the Rephaim...

notes.title: Ancient peoples
notes.body: Rephaim, Zuzim, and Emim are old population names, some associated with giant or mighty people traditions.

verses.restored: and the Horites in their hill country of Seir, as far as El-Paran, which is by the wilderness.

familiar.text: And the Horites in their mount Seir, unto Elparan, which is by the wilderness.

verses.restored: Then they turned back and came to En-Mishpat, that is, Kadesh, and struck all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who dwelled in Hazazon-Tamar.

familiar.text: And they returned, and came to Enmishpat, which is Kadesh...

verses.restored: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, that is Zoar, went out, and they arranged battle against them in the Valley of Siddim,

familiar.text: And there went out the king of Sodom, and the king of Gomorrah...

verses.restored: against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar: four kings against five.

familiar.text: With Chedorlaomer the king of Elam... four kings with five.

verses.restored: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits. The kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled and fell there, and those who remained fled to the hill country.

familiar.text: And the vale of Siddim was full of slimepits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled...

verses.restored: They took all the possessions of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food, and they went away.

familiar.text: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.

verses.restored: They also took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son, and his possessions, and went away. He was dwelling in Sodom.

familiar.text: And they took Lot, Abram's brother's son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods...

notes.title: Lot in Sodom
notes.body: Lot’s movement toward Sodom in Genesis 13 now becomes direct vulnerability inside Sodom’s fate.

verses.restored: One who escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew. He was dwelling by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner; they were allies of Abram.

familiar.text: And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew...

notes.title: Abram the Hebrew
notes.body: This is the first use of Hebrew for Abram. The term ivri may relate to Eber or to crossing over.

verses.restored: When Abram heard that his brother had been captured, he led out his trained men, born in his house, three hundred eighteen, and pursued as far as Dan.

familiar.text: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained servants...

notes.title: Brother
notes.body: Lot is Abram’s nephew, but kinship language calls him brother here, emphasizing familial duty.

verses.restored: He divided himself against them by night, he and his servants, and struck them and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.

familiar.text: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them...

verses.restored: He brought back all the possessions, and also brought back Lot his brother and his possessions, and the women and the people.

familiar.text: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought again his brother Lot...

notes.title: Recovery
notes.body: Abram’s rescue restores people and possessions, including women who had been taken in war.

verses.restored: After he returned from striking Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh, that is, the King’s Valley.

familiar.text: And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer...

verses.restored: Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine. He was priest of El Elyon, God Most High.

familiar.text: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he was the priest of the most high God.

notes.title: Melchizedek
notes.body: The name means king of righteousness or my king is righteousness. He is both king of Salem and priest of El Elyon.

notes.title: El Elyon
notes.body: El Elyon means God Most High, a divine title that Abram explicitly identifies with the Eternal in verse 22.

verses.restored: He blessed him and said, Blessed be Abram by El Elyon, Maker of heavens and earth.

familiar.text: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed be Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:

notes.title: Maker/Possessor
notes.body: Qoneh can mean acquiring, possessing, or creating/making. Maker of heavens and earth fits the blessing context.

verses.restored: And blessed be El Elyon, who has delivered your enemies into your hand. And Abram gave him a tenth of everything.

familiar.text: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. And he gave him tithes of all.

notes.title: Tenth
notes.body: Abram gives a tenth to Melchizedek after the blessing. Later tithe traditions develop beyond this scene.

verses.restored: The king of Sodom said to Abram, Give me the persons, and take the possessions for yourself.

familiar.text: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.

verses.restored: Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lifted my hand to the Eternal, El Elyon, Maker of heavens and earth,

familiar.text: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the LORD, the most high God...

notes.title: YHWH and El Elyon
notes.body: Abram identifies the Eternal with El Elyon, the title used by Melchizedek.

verses.restored: that I will not take a thread or sandal strap or anything that is yours, lest you say, I made Abram rich.

familiar.text: That I will not take from a thread even to a shoelatchet... lest thou shouldest say, I have made Abram rich:

notes.title: Refusal of Sodom’s wealth
notes.body: Abram refuses enrichment from Sodom, contrasting with Genesis 12 where he gained wealth through Sarai’s danger in Egypt.

verses.restored: Nothing for me except what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me: Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. Let them take their share.

familiar.text: Save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men which went with me...

notes.title: Share for allies
notes.body: Abram refuses profit for himself but preserves the rightful share of his allies.
